title: rosa canina subvar. dolosa
intro: Rosa canina subvar. dolosa, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This shrub is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. dolosa include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as supporting skin and cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in creams and ointments to treat various skin conditions.
